title: Harness Operational Traces — Ozi writes to PathGraph after every operation
what: Every Ozi operation (lug pickup, teaching adoption, migration step, advisor creation, lug complete) appends a structured trace entry to WAI-Spoke/pathgraph/history.jsonl. The trace is the canonical self-documentation record — the system generates its own operational history without manual intervention. Each entry carries: ts (ISO timestamp), op_type (operation category), lug_id (the lug being executed), outcome (success|failure|partial), delta (what changed from plan), teaching_candidate (bool — did this surface a teaching candidate?). The append is mandatory: no trace means the operation did not happen from the system's perspective.
why: Without traces, the system cannot observe its own operations. There is no way to detect recurring failure patterns, aspiration drift (planned X, got Y), or teaching candidates that arise from successful resolutions. PathGraph history.jsonl becomes the canonical self-documentation record that feeds three consumers: Navigator (fleet trace queries for desired-state decisions), Historian (aspiration drift extraction from delta fields), and Ozi itself (recurring delta detection triggers improvement lugs at threshold >= 3 fleet-wide occurrences). Without this protocol, self-improvement and goal alignment loops have no substrate to operate on.

use_cases.title: Ozi completing a lug
use_cases.trigger: Ozi marks any lug complete (success, failure, or partial)
use_cases.outcome: Trace entry appended to WAI-Spoke/pathgraph/history.jsonl before the lug status file is updated; entry includes outcome and delta describing any deviation from the lug's stated implementation_notes

use_cases.title: Historian querying fleet traces
use_cases.trigger: Historian advisor runs aspiration drift analysis across fleet PathGraphs
use_cases.outcome: Historian reads history.jsonl files across all spokes; extracts delta fields; identifies recurring patterns where planned behavior diverged from actual; surfaces summary to Navigator for desired-state recalibration

use_cases.title: Navigator detecting recurring delta patterns
use_cases.trigger: Navigator reviews fleet traces at wakeup or consolidation
use_cases.outcome: Navigator identifies delta types appearing >= 3 times across fleet; generates improvement lug for each recurring pattern; improvement lug routed to the spoke or harness layer responsible for the root cause

use_cases.title: Teaching candidate surfaced from operation
use_cases.trigger: Ozi completes an operation with a non-empty delta and sets teaching_candidate = true
use_cases.outcome: Ozi writes a teaching candidate to WAI-Spoke/generate-teaching/candidates/ with source field set to the op_type that triggered it; candidate enters standard peer review queue

patterns.name: Append-only trace log
patterns.description: history.jsonl is append-only — never overwritten or truncated by Ozi. Each session produces zero or more new lines. The file is the durable operational record. Rotation (archiving lines older than N days) is a separate harness hygiene concern and must not be triggered by Ozi during normal operations.

patterns.name: Delta as teaching signal
patterns.description: The delta field is the primary signal for self-improvement. An empty delta means the operation went exactly as planned. A non-empty delta means something diverged — the description of that divergence is the raw material for teaching candidates and improvement lugs. Ozi should write the delta in plain language: 'Expected to find X, found Y' or 'Step 3 skipped because condition Z was false'.

patterns.name: Trace before completion
patterns.description: The trace entry is written before the lug is marked complete. This ordering guarantee means that if the session ends mid-operation, the incomplete trace (with outcome: partial) is the system's record that the operation started but did not finish cleanly. An agent picking up the lug next session will find the partial trace and know to re-audit before resuming.

how.trigger: Any Ozi operation reaches its completion point — success, failure, or partial abort
steps: 1. Ozi reaches the terminal state of any operation (lug complete, teaching adopted, migration step finished, advisor created)

2. Ozi constructs the trace entry: ts = now (ISO), op_type = one of lug_pickup|teaching_adoption|migration_step|advisor_creation|lug_complete, lug_id = active lug id, outcome = success|failure|partial, delta = plain-language description of divergence from plan (empty string if none), teaching_candidate = true if delta is non-empty and Ozi judges the divergence is generalizable
3. Ozi appends the JSON-serialized trace entry as a single line to WAI-Spoke/pathgraph/history.jsonl (create file if absent)

4. If teaching_candidate == true and delta is non-empty: Ozi writes a teaching candidate stub to WAI-Spoke/generate-teaching/candidates/{lug_id}-trace-candidate.json with fields: source (op_type), lug_id, delta, ts, status: draft
5. Ozi updates the lug status (marks complete / moves file) only after the trace write succeeds
constraints: Trace MUST be written before lug is marked complete — no exceptions
No trace = operation did not happen from the system's perspective; a subsequent agent will re-run the operation
delta field must be a plain string — no nested objects; encode structured data as a readable sentence
teaching_candidate may only be true if delta is non-empty; a clean operation cannot produce a teaching candidate via this mechanism
history.jsonl is append-only; Ozi never deletes, truncates, or rewrites lines

triggers: Lug pickup — Ozi reads and begins executing a lug from incoming/
Teaching adoption — Ozi applies a teaching to spoke skill files
Migration step — Ozi completes one step of a migration lug audit sequence
Advisor creation — Ozi spawns a specialized advisor for a Navigator-flagged concern
Lug complete — Ozi marks any lug done (success, failure, or partial)
not_when: Read-only wakeup scans that do not modify any file
Navigator query operations (reads fleet state but does not execute a lug)
Historian analysis passes (reads history.jsonl but does not write to it)
Session guard checks and hook validation — these are not Ozi lug operations
when.frequency: One trace entry per operation terminal event. A single session may produce zero (wakeup-only sessions) to many (batch impl sessions) trace entries.

inputs: Active lug being executed — provides lug_id and implementation_notes for delta comparison
Ozi's assessment of outcome and divergence — outcome and delta fields
outputs: WAI-Spoke/pathgraph/history.jsonl — one appended line per operation: {ts, op_type, lug_id, outcome, delta, teaching_candidate}
state_changes: history.jsonl line count increases by 1 per trace
Teaching candidate file created in generate-teaching/candidates/ when teaching_candidate == true
No changes to lug files until after trace write completes

constraints: Trace entry must be valid JSON serializable to a single line (no unescaped newlines in delta field)
op_type must be one of the five enumerated values: lug_pickup, teaching_adoption, migration_step, advisor_creation, lug_complete
outcome must be one of: success, failure, partial
teaching_candidate must be a boolean (not a string)
history.jsonl lives at WAI-Spoke/pathgraph/history.jsonl — not at any hub-relative or cross-spoke path; each node owns its own trace log

tests.description: Execute a test lug to completion; verify that history.jsonl contains an entry with matching lug_id and that the lug's status file was updated after the trace line's ts timestamp
tests.acceptance: history.jsonl has one new line with correct lug_id; lug file modification time is >= trace ts

tests.description: Simulate a session ending mid-lug; verify that history.jsonl contains an entry with outcome: partial and that a subsequent agent session finds the partial trace and re-audits before resuming
tests.acceptance: history.jsonl contains partial entry; next session agent log references the partial trace

tests.description: Execute a lug where the implementation diverges from plan (non-empty delta); verify that a teaching candidate stub appears in generate-teaching/candidates/ with source matching the op_type
tests.acceptance: Candidate file exists at expected path; source field matches op_type; status is draft

tests.description: Execute a lug where outcome is success and delta is empty string; verify that no teaching candidate file is written
tests.acceptance: No new files in generate-teaching/candidates/ after clean operation

perceive: Read this spec (spec-harness-operational-traces-v1). Read the parent harness spec (spec-harness-self-maintaining-wheel-v1) for architectural context. Review WAI-Spoke/pathgraph/ to understand current PathGraph structure. Read spec-harness-goal-affinity-v1 for how Navigator consumes trace data. Read spec-harness-kb-v1 for how KB entries interact with trace-based teaching candidates.
execute: ROUTING: Ozi implementation lives in tools/ozi_autopilot.py which is Basher-owned. NEVER edit tools/ directly (CLAUDE.md anti-pattern). Implementation path: create an impl lug (routed_to: BASHER) and deliver it to basher/WAI-Spoke/lugs/incoming/. The lug's implementation_notes must describe the full change: (1) locate the lug-complete handler in tools/ozi_autopilot.py; (2) add trace construction logic after outcome is determined — build dict {ts: ISO now, op_type: from enum, lug_id: active lug id, outcome: success|failure|partial, delta: plain string, teaching_candidate: bool}; (3) append using python3 json.dumps to history.jsonl (open append mode); (4) if teaching_candidate == true and delta non-empty: write candidate stub to WAI-Spoke/generate-teaching/candidates/{lug_id}-trace-candidate.json; (5) only update lug status file after trace write succeeds. Create the Basher impl lug first — do not make any direct edits to tools/.
verify: history.jsonl grows by one line per Ozi operation. Partial-outcome entries appear when a lug is abandoned mid-session. Teaching candidate files appear in generate-teaching/candidates/ only when delta is non-empty. No operation completes without a trace entry. Navigator can read history.jsonl and extract op_type distribution without error.
